# pgmpy
> pgmpy is a Python library for causal inference, probabilistic modeling, Bayesian networks, and directed acyclic graphs.

## Pages

### Home
- [Key Features](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/index.html): pgmpy documentation for causal discovery, model testing, causal effect estimation, parameter estimation, probabilistic and causal inference, and simulations in Python.

### Getting Started
- [Getting Started](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/started/index.html): Start using pgmpy with installation instructions, a quickstart workflow with minimal examples for each task, and pgmpy's license.
- [Installation](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/started/install.html): pgmpy supports Python 3.10 through 3.14. For most users, the base PyPI install is enough:
- [Quickstart](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/started/quickstart.html): Task-oriented examples to help you get started with pgmpy.
- [Contributing to pgmpy](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/started/contributing.html): Welcome — and thanks for helping make **pgmpy** better! Use the quick links below for common actions, then read the short guides that follow.
- [License](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/started/license.html): The MIT License (MIT)

### Guides
- [User Guide](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/documentation.html): Task-oriented pgmpy guides for causal discovery, parameter estimation, probabilistic inference, simulations, and model building.
- [Causal Discovery and Structure Learning](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/causal_discovery.html): Learn causal graphs from data using the unified causal discovery APIs in pgmpy.
- [Causal Estimation](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/causal_estimation.html): Estimate causal effects from graphs and data using pgmpy's causal inference and prediction APIs.
- [Causal Identification](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/causal_identification.html): Identify adjustment and frontdoor strategies from causal graphs using pgmpy's identification APIs.
- [Defining a Custom Model](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/custom_model.html): Build custom pgmpy models and attach the right CPD or factor types for your workflow.
- [Example Datasets](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/datasets.html): Discover and load built-in benchmark datasets using pgmpy's dataset helpers.
- [Example Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/example_models.html): Discover and load built-in example models using pgmpy's example-model helpers.
- [Extensibility](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/extensibility.html): Extend pgmpy using the repository templates for new datasets, models, metrics, and discovery algorithms.
- [Exporting / Importing Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/io.html): Read and write pgmpy models using the model-level save/load helpers and readwrite classes.
- [Metrics](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/metrics.html): Evaluate learned graphs and fitted models using pgmpy's metric APIs.
- [Parameter Estimation](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/parameter_estimation.html): Estimate model parameters from data using pgmpy's unified parameter-estimation APIs.
- [Plotting Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/plotting.html): Visualize pgmpy graphs with Graphviz, daft, or networkx drawing tools.
- [Probabilistic Inference](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/probabilistic_inference.html): Query fitted pgmpy models using the unified probabilistic inference APIs.
- [Simulations](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/guides/simulations.html): Generate synthetic data from fitted pgmpy models using the simulation APIs.

### Examples
- [Example Notebooks](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/examples.html): Example notebooks for pgmpy covering Bayesian networks, structure learning, inference, simulations, and causal analysis.

### API Reference
- [API Reference](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/reference.html): Public API reference for pgmpy graphs, models, factors, inference, discovery, metrics, datasets, example models, and model import or export.
- [Graph Classes](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/base.html): Core graph representations used by pgmpy models, discovery algorithms, and causal workflows.
- [Causal Inference](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/causal_inference.html): Public classes for identification, interventional queries, and regression-based causal effect estimation.
- [Conditional Independence (CI) Tests](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/ci_test.html): Selector utilities and built-in CI test implementations.
- [Datasets and Example Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/data.html): Built-in data and example-model discovery helpers.
- [Factors and CPDs](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/factors.html): Factor, CPD, and factor-container classes used to parameterize pgmpy models.
- [Independencies](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/independencies.html): Classes for representing independence assertions and collections of independencies.
- [Inference and Sampling](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/inference.html): 
- [Metrics](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/metrics.html): Metrics for evaluating learned structures, implied independencies, and fitted models.
- [Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/models.html): Public model classes for probabilistic, causal, and structural-equation workflows.
- [Parameter Estimation](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/parameter_estimation.html): Estimator classes for fitting model parameters and related marginal representations from data.
- [Reading/Writing](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/readwrite.html): Reader and writer classes for pgmpy-supported interchange formats.
- [Causal Discovery and Structure Learning](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/structure_learning.html): Discovery algorithms and expert-guided workflows for learning graph structure from data.
- [Structure Scoring](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/structure_score.html): Structure score classes used by score-based discovery workflows.
- [Undirected and Derived Models](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/api/undirected.html): Convenience index for the undirected-model subset of the full :doc:`Models </api/models>` reference.

### Project
- [Getting Involved](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/development.html): Contributing, issue reporting, pull requests, and community channels for the pgmpy project.
- [Citation](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/citation.html): If you use pgmpy in your scientific work, please consider citing us:
- [Roadmap](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/roadmap.html): High-level roadmap themes for pgmpy development and contribution areas.
- [Tutorial Notebooks](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/tutorial.html): Long-form pgmpy tutorial notebooks covering probabilistic graphical models, Bayesian networks, inference, learning, and case studies.

## Machine-readable

- [Sitemap](https://pgmpy.org/v1.1/sitemap.xml): XML sitemap for crawlers and site indexing.
